programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Losing custody of two children due to neglect, donating blood for money, and alleged domestic violence come into play when ex-lovers fight(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A 'ride from hell' is how a man describes his ex-girlfriend's driving during an alleged fit of jealousy, but admits to taking money from her after the incident(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Dangerous living conditions, a grandmother's visitation rights and robbery are disputed(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman's bald spots caused by allegedly faulty hair extensions applied by a stylist who was accused of drinking on the job are disputed(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin(n)

programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: Siobhan has a hard time in Liverpool sorting out a family on a delayed flight who have missed their connection to Belfast, while passengers at Luton learn their flight has been postponed for two days by a freak snowfall in Athens. Duty manager Alan Darbyshire hears good news about his kidney transplant and easyJet owner Stelios looks forward to the announcement of his company's first year of trading on the stock market(n)

programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: Purser Mark Hobson holds an impromptu in-flight quiz in preparation for his appearance on a TV show, a determined businesswoman takes extreme measures to get to her meeting in Glasgow after missing her plane and a girl with an expired passport makes her flight with a minute to spare(n)

programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: Purser Vicki Cox's boyfriend Nigel sneaks aboard a routine flight to ask for her hand in marriage. Pilots Mike Jamieson and Tony Underwood get to grips with the airport's simulator, and airline boss Stelios is asked to come to the aid of a wedding speaker who has missed his flight to Aberdeen(n)

programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: Pilot Mike Jameson plays Father Christmas for 40 Luton schoolchildren, but festive cheer is scarce on the ground as anxious travellers struggle to reach their families in time for the holiday. An Inverness-bound mother of twins discovers to her horror that company policy demands one adult per child, and a Liverpool traveller tries to cut through red tape to join his parents in Geneva(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: A teenager is brainwashed by one parent to help murder the other parent(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: The prime suspect in the murder of an American teacher while living in Seoul, South Korea, in 1988 speaks publicly about the case for the first time in 30 years(n)

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: When a friendly woman is murdered inside her home, police suspect her killer is an ex-lover. But after two years and a series of informants, police discover the real perpetrators(n)

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: A woman kills a tenant and a landlord after the landlord offered her free rent to take care of his troublesome tenants(n)

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls 'kill kits'(n)

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The officers find themselves stuck in Kenya's Makindi province, struggling to find a safe pick up point. Meanwhile, Dave and Donna are unsure about their future together(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Jeremy announces he is planning to marry his girlfriend, but is surprised at his friends' response. Meanwhile, Cate Hobbs receives bad news(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack officially opens the newly rebuilt station, while Adam's grief at the loss of his entire family in a fatal car crash is compounded by suggestions that wife Denise drove through a red light while talking on her mobile phone. Phil tempts fate by flirting with Samantha, while Suzie monitors a recently released child abuser, but makes the mistake of contacting his daughter - who is so distressed that she attempts suicide shortly afterwards(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Abra returns to Holby with his nephew, who needs an urgent transplant. Ric is unable to operate because of the injury to his hand - but he relents when the patient's mother reveals a secret. Owen fears he might get sacked for hitting Amy's stepfather, thereby jeopardising his access to Katie. Diane tries to keep Laura from turning on her ex, but when she learns about his clandestine relationship with Chrissie, tragedy strikes(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sean faces a tough time as an injured fireman and his pregnant girlfriend land him with a moral dilemma. Penny helps Josh cope with his gambling addiction and George has a bad day as increasing pressure from Max forces her to burn the midnight oil. Medical drama, starring Derek Thompson, Gerald Kyd and Ian Bleasdale(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry races to Ramani's house, where an increasingly unbalanced Margaret Barnes is planning to commit suicide - only to be stabbed and critically injured while attempting to wrest a knife from her. Smithy takes JT to task for spreading gossip around the station, while Dan and Tony try to track down an inventive graffiti artist who uses cleaning products to produce negative images on dirty walls around Sun Hill. It's certainly a novel approach - but does it really constitute a crime?(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon and Phil's infatuation with each other is starting to affect the pub and garage as they are devoting more time to each other rather then their work(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil visits Grant in prison and tells him Ricky is going off to work for Frank. Aiden learns he may not be able to play again(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Ariadne pleads with Nick to marry her, Billy is setting Kevin up with Tina, and it seems Patty and Hallam might be hitting it off. Later, a policeman is stuck in a muddy pool(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: As everyone comes to terms with the shocking events, Lexie fears Hector's dire prediction may come true and the wedding will be jinxed. Her anxieties are increased when her mother arrives at Glenbogle in dramatic fashion, immediately treading on Molly's toes by taking control. Alastair Mackenzie, Dawn Steele and Susan Hampshire star(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David plans a surprise party for Karen's 40th birthday, even though she'd rather not bother, and Anna rekindles her romance with Simon now he's returned from Australia a single man once more. Jess is devastated to receive disappointing A level results, meaning she can't get away from home, and newcomers the Morecroft family arrive in Skelthwaite, hoping to shake off their tragic past(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Nellie ensures Aveline and her first granddaughter are brought home in style. Leonora continues to fascinate the Boswell boys(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The mystery of the body in the swimming pool continues. Anxious not to alarm the guests, Clive orders the pool lights to be turned off so that the corpse cannot be seen - but instead Fred and the Yellowcoats plunge the whole camp into darkness(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: In an attempt to spite Lionel, Jean decides to make a play for his publisher Alistair. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In a bid to persuade Pearl to take back her errant husband, Howard's friends try to convince her he is living as a tramp - but he is far from happy at the prospect of going it alone in the wilderness. Meanwhile, Toby's attempt to regain Monica's affections is thwarted by a dog, and Marina decides to dress down for a change. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch helps an Italian detective recover a shipload of stolen antiquities and Brackenreid considers shipping out altogether(n)

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: The medic investigates the death of a strangled young gypsy girl, and must tread carefully as prejudice rears its head. Drama, starring Craig MacLachlan(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The appeal of a man convicted of murder and mutilation forces Grace to question the decision she made on the case 30 years ago. Someone who appears to be copying his chilling tactics sends the team footage of a new victim having his finger cut off. At the same time, Grace receives threats and a macabre package - a jar of pickled, severed fingers. Sue Johnston and Trevor Eve star(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry races to Ramani's house, where an increasingly unbalanced Margaret Barnes is planning to commit suicide - only to be stabbed and critically injured while attempting to wrest a knife from her. Smithy takes JT to task for spreading gossip around the station, while Dan and Tony try to track down an inventive graffiti artist who uses cleaning products to produce negative images on dirty walls around Sun Hill. It's certainly a novel approach - but does it really constitute a crime?(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Ariadne pleads with Nick to marry her, Billy is setting Kevin up with Tina, and it seems Patty and Hallam might be hitting it off. Later, a policeman is stuck in a muddy pool(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina tries to quell suspicion about her unexplained absences by going on the beat with Amber and Dan - only to be punched hard in the stomach by a suspect. Smithy reprimands Margaret and Roger for gossiping about Ramani's state of mind, and Honey prepares to lie on Scott's behalf in a bid to get him out of jail, while Lance pledges to leave no stone unturned in his quest to secure incriminating evidence against gangster Adi Mateen(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gabriel and Sheelagh put their kiss behind them and work as a team, while Honey's wedding day ends in a brawl, forcing the registrar to abandon the ceremony. Smithy frets over Gina's refusal to take things easy, and Adam suffers a personal tragedy, leaving him racked with guilt at putting his job before the demands and responsibilities of family life(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives move into their new premises following the explosion that wrecked the old Nonnatus House, and are joined by fresh arrival Sister Winifred - a kindly-but-naive young woman. Jenny becomes concerned about new mother Merle's mental state and the welfare of her baby, who isn't feeding properly. Dr Turner believes it could be something more serious - although the mystery is eventually solved by the last person anyone would have expected. Chummy is struggling to adjust to her new role as a housewife and mother, and organises an open day at the community centre. Jessica Raine, Stephen McGann and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie's feathers are ruffled when Jenny is promoted, but the new Acting Sister has more to worry about after a mother-to-be reveals the baby she is carrying was not fathered by her husband - and to complicate matters, the real father is black. Cynthia is inspired by a lecture on natural childbirth, convinced it will help an overly anxious patient, while Alec and his friends try to persuade Jenny to join them on a CND march. Jessica Raine, Helen George and Bryony Hannah star in the 1950s-set nursing drama(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Crabtree and John are transporting a convicted axe murderer to prison when she escapes to exact revenge on those who testified against her(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: An alleged sexual assault requires delicate handling. The boat where the attack took place belongs to a friend of the Commissioner, forcing the detectives to go undercover at a fancy dress party as they attempt to arrest their suspect. With only circumstantial evidence and a victim of unreliable character, Ray surprises his colleagues by proving he has a gentler side, gaining the confidence of a young witness(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene comes up against a socialist feminist group when a man's death he initially treated as suicide becomes the subject of a murder inquiry. Chris uses the arrest of every member as an opportunity to impress Shaz with his knowledge of women's issues, while Ray dates a communist. An intriguing code found by Alex and deciphered by the desk sergeant leads the team closer to the truth(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A respected Polish-born doctor is prosecuted for his alleged involvement in Nazi war crimes during the Second World War, forcing victims of persecution to relive their harrowing experiences in concentration camps. Kavanagh finds it difficult to remain professionally detached from such an emotive case and discovers that, after 50 years, witness testimony is not always reliable. Legal drama, starring John Thaw, Frederick Treves, Warren Mitchell and Peter Firth(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth delves into a suspicious death, while loyal sidekick Geoffrey and her put-upon husband Robert become involved in a delicate undercover operation. While the police seem satisfied that there was no foul play, the dead man's brother believes that the housekeeper is a fraud who has systematically stolen property from him(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth is called upon to mediate in a dispute at a condemned block of flats, and soon discovers the man responsible for their construction has a shady past. Further investigations send the part-time sleuth on a tense race against time as she strives to prevent the cowboy builder from profiting from his shoddy work. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Robert begins a career as a journalist and Geoffrey joins a gym to help Hetty solve a case for her hairdresser, whose errant husband has a lot to hide following an apparently random attack in a gents' toilet(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. Her husband struggles to cope with his daughter's needs, but the sisters rally round to support the family. Trixie teaches a keep fit class which results in a medical emergency, leaving her determined to teach her patients to understand their own bodies(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara treats a pregnant woman whose dockworker husband is struggling to support her as opportunities for work dry up. She is forced into a difficult decision that leaves Barbara facing the biggest test of her career. Nurse Crane meets a kindred spirit when she learns to speak Spanish, and Sister Evangelina helps a woman with breastfeeding, leading to unforeseen consequences(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A college principal is crushed to death in the shaft of a lift that has been tampered with, and suspicion falls on his widow. However, Ross believes the man's deputy was behind the fatality and sets out to prove his guilt. Meanwhile, Fraser traces his biological mother, but is disappointed by her response when he contacts her(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The body of a private detective is discovered at the bottom of a local quarry. After learning from the victim's fiancee that he was working on the case of a missing woman, and had claimed to have found her, the team becomes convinced his investigation is the key to solving the murder. Starring Blythe Duff and John Michie(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth goes to North Yorkshire to meet her long-lost cousin Kate, and ends up investigating a case of demonic visitations. Amiable Geoffrey joins her in an attempt to provide moral support - and is soon drawn into his own little mystery(n)

programme.title: Olobobs

programme.desc: Norman is forced to do a spot of cleaning after a trip to the lighthouse goes wrong(n)

programme.desc: A snooty king and queen invite themselves round for dinner to King Rhi and Queen Rhiannon's castle to sample Magi Hud's cooking. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover(n)

Starring Ricky Gervais, Ashley Jensen and Stephen Merchant(n)

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Hutchesons' Grammar School in Glasgow(n)

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Kathy Jones leads a service for Passion Sunday from Bangor Cathedral in north Wales. With hymns sung by a Songs of Praise congregation recorded in March 2018(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell casts her eye over a two-bed flat in Battersea. Martin Roberts visits a linked-detached house in Winsford, Cheshire, and a property in Stourbridge, West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones speaks to Archbishop of York designate the Rt Rev Stephen Cottrell about the Christian hope that can still be found despite the current troubled times. Aled also refelcts on recent editions of the programme in which he met those whose faith saw them through especially challenging times - inlcuding a woman who survived the Grenfell Tower tragedy, and a Second World War veteran inolved in the D-Day landings(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: City-dwelling families, couples and individuals set out to find their ideal rural retreats with the help of a property expert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action(n)

programme.title: Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted (2012)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals continue their quest to get back to their New York home. Having made it to Monte Carlo, they join a circus on a European tour as a means of travelling undetected - but an animal control agent is in hot pursuit. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Siblings from Wolverhampton are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill. Sommer and Daryl's large family gatherings revolve around shared meals and they were both taught to cook by their grandmother, who cheers them on from the audience. Fiercely competitive, Sommer intends to beat her little brother, but Daryl is determined he can get the better of his sister. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, Steve Backshall discovers why the Bahamas provide a sanctuary for sharks, looking at the protection offered to them, people's changing attitudes and the key role science plays in their conservation. He joins researchers who have dedicated their lives to this unique and often misunderstood group of animals and learns that a healthy shark population is absolutely vital to a healthy marine ecosystem(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lugg Valley in Herefordshire to hunt for signs of spring, joining volunteers making sure toads can make it safely across main roads to their favoured spawning grounds. She also meets people who have just planted out their first forest garden and chats to an artist who sees the beauty in the flooded landscape(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to Morden Hall Park in South London, where items include letters written by Mother Teresa and a bronze by South African sculptor Anton Van Wouw. There is also a colourful collage by celebrated British artist and illustrator John Minton, and a huge collection of intricately designed silver vinaigrette boxes(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: Three months have passed and Kaya is living with Dan and Emily and enjoying her new lifestyle, but James has his reservations and is concerned that she is being exploited. Souter is suspicious Kaya's background doesn't quite check out and is having to field questions from young and ambitious journalist Eleanor. At home tensions between the couple and their surrogate are at breaking point, and Dan questions what he really knows about the teenager he has invited into his home. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Rain Man (1988)
programme.desc: Brash yuppie car dealer Charlie discovers his deceased father has left the family fortune to Raymond, the autistic elder brother he never knew he had. In a bid to get his hands on the money, he tracks down and abducts Raymond - but the ensuing cross-country journey proves to be a profound learning experience. Oscar-winning drama, starring Tom Cruise and Dustin Hoffman, with Valeria Golino, Gerald R Molen and Jack Murdock(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Charlie also travels back in time to explore crime and punishment at Beaumaris Gaol(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Couple Nicole and Lewis compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Mike Reid and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Billy Bragg pays the Arderns a visit as they get to grips with 1960s life. The new decade brings a dizzying number of new developments, including skiffle music, hula hoops and sliced bread. It also brought about a seismic change in shopping with the introduction of self-service. The rise of supermarkets sees the personal service of the Arderns' shop under threat, forcing them to diversify their stock. The 1960s saw Sheffield becoming home to new workers from across the Commonwealth, many of whom share their memories of the era and the importance of the local corner shop to shift workers(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter E, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg heads to the Big Pit Mining Museum in south Wales, where he goes underground to learn about the limited safety equipment used in the 1930s. Vicky Stradling describes one of the worst colliery disasters in history, and David Bogg investigates the occupational hazards of working with pit ponies(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: The week-long battle between antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey nears its end, but which of the pair will triumph is not yet clear. On the final day, the duo fight it out across four different locations, before racing towards the finish line at the Showdown Auction in Essex(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: London cabbie Mason McQueen and Terry Wogan head to Marlow, where they are intrigued to discover the historic Buckinghamshire town boasts many food producers and restaurants. The duo are served up a dish by Michelin-starred chef Atul Kochhar, treated to a pub lunch by Tom Kerridge and after Mason has a go at rowing on the River Thames, it's time to sample the county's regional dish of Bacon Badger(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Professional and personal pressures push Superintendent Dunne to breaking point, and Ash is appalled to see where her suspicions have led(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The mission to find an executive head chef for Lake District restaurant The Gilpin continues. The last three candidates prepare a partridge fit for a fine-dining menu - and with only one place left in tomorrow's final, the pressure is on. While one of the chefs sets out to create something deserving of a Michelin star, another has trouble convincing the family he really wants the position, when it turns out he never stays in one job very long(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Gok Wan joins Brian Conley to revisit some classic television series of his formative years. From the magic and mystery of Japanese children's drama Monkey, to the pan-European quiz show Going for Gold, via groundbreaking, anarchic pop series The Tube, these were the shows that apparently helped shape Gok into the style icon that he is today(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, Anita Manning discovers a Victorian dynamometer used for measuring the pressure of steam and David Harper is taken back to the days of ocean liners with a wine stopper from 1912. Presenter Paul Martin visits Cromer to find out about the RNLI's most decorated lifeboatman(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The week's winner is crowned, as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright take on a series of challenging quiz games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, Michael Portillo investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic. Then, in Plymouth, he uncovers what happened to surviving crew members of the most famous ocean liner in history, the Titanic. Later, at Fowey, the former politician rediscovers a lost literary figure known as Q, who immortalised the town in his novels(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef explores Burgundy and the Auvergne, sampling a sorrel soup made by a chateau owner from the produce of the garden and showcasing Salers beef and Cantal cheese. Back in his Provencal country kitchen, Rick prepares Cantal Bourriols - buckwheat pancakes made with farmhouse eggs and Cep mushrooms(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring London and the South-east chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the one-time Blue Peter presenter Konnie Huq, whose Cookie and the Most Annoying Boy in the World adventure series is making waves with young readers(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme celebrates the humble snowdrop on a visit to one of the world's largest collections, lovingly nurtured in a suburban back garden, as Monty Don offers tips on what to do once they have finished flowering. At Longmeadow it's time to get dahlias into growth and, for inspiration, there's camera footage of a field full of dahlias in Sussex along with tips from a professional grower. Plus, Nick Bailey revels in the beauty of magnolias and Monty highlights the jobs to be getting on with this weekend(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Journalist Adrian Chiles, former politician Edwina Currie, Olympian Fatima Whitbread, broadcaster Mim Shaikh, TV presenter Amar Latif, comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn set out to cover sections of the Sultans Trail - a modern-day 2,200km pilgrimage across Eastern Europe that starts in Vienna and ends in Istanbul. Each has varying religions, with Dom and Pauline confirmed atheists. Starting in Serbia's capital Belgrade, they stay in basic hostels and sleep in shared dorms, acting as simple pilgrims traversing the ancient military route by rail and foot(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sally Phillips, Ross Noble and Nish Kumar join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Odorous and Odious'. During the course of the show, the panellists learn why the world's second-smelliest man is so frustrated. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: The Lost City of Z (2016)
programme.desc: Drama telling the true story of British explorer Col Percy Fawcett, who in the 1920s told his fellows at the Royal Geographical Society in London he had found evidence of a secret city hidden away in the Amazon. But proving his theory meant returning yet again. Charlie Hunnam, Robert Pattinson and Sienna Miller star(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus Daily Update: BBC News Special
programme.desc: The latest reports about Covid-19(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: It has been a week where life in Britain has changed beyond recognition. Schools have shut down, supermarket shelves have emptied, and everyone has been told to stay at home where possible. Meanwhile, the number of deaths is rising and hospitals are preparing for many more Covid-19 patients. Jane Corbin looks at how Britain is coping with its biggest crisis since the Second World War - and asks if the Government has the right strategy to contain the virus(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown nears a conclusion. With one suspect outstanding, one on bail and one in custody, can the team of detectives and specialists secure enough evidence to charge them with murder?(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including a toad that grows its babies on its back, and a two-headed snake(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed joins a whole host of CBBC megastars on another amazing adventure All Over the UK!(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall returns to find the deadliest animals in Australia, focusing on saltwater crocodiles, snakes and the box jellyfish(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Your Food
programme.desc: James Wong and Michael Mosley explore the way the brain can generate cravings to influence diets. James discovers the source of a food that invariably has a profound hold over the brain - fat-rich chocolate. Its primary ingredient is cocoa butter, which has a very characteristic effect on a set of receptors on the tongue. To understand these receptors, Michael examines a cream produced from the rich milk of water buffalo that has a similar effect. The duo also explore the hold that caffeinated drinks can have and take on some of the hottest chillies on the planet to show what the burn does to the brain(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Pirate Fairy (2014)
programme.desc: A rebellious fairy steals the magic pixie dust that grants her people the power of flight and runs away, becoming captain of a band of pirates. Tinker Bell and her friends set off to recover the dust, but succumb to a spell that switches their magical powers. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Christina Hendricks and Tom Hiddleston(n)

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: Part two of two. Liz Bonnin concludes her search for animal intelligence as she encounters grey whales that can display emotions. She also meets Caribbean monkeys able to outwit humans, and communicates with a chimp. The programme also explores the behaviour of Seattle's crows, how bright dogs are capable of learning up to 1,000 words, and why Asian elephants spend hours looking at their reflections in giant mirrors(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Kerth Gumbs, Steve Groves, Karl O'Dell and Ivan Tisdall-Downes compete in the heats for the London and South East region. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a dish layering textures of carrot and goats cheese inspired by Beatrix Potter, and a hazelnut dish celebrating author Julia Donaldson's acclaimed picture book, The Gruffalo. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three London and South East chefs try to impress as they serve up their main courses and desserts inspired by children's literature, with one of the contestants meet War Horse author Michael Morpurgo. Veteran judge Tommy Banks then blind tastes and rank their mini-puddings, before the dessert proper sees dishes inspired by One Snowy Night, The Faraway Tree and Winnie the Pooh(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Piers Taylor helps to transform a small three-bedroom house in West London for couple Skye and Matt, who have four children and a fifth on the way. He must find low cost ways to use every inch of their home, and comes up with a series of sleeping platforms that could utilise wasted space in the loft. Meanwhile, Adam Khan finds an ingenious way of a splitting an ordinary home into two for Mary and her adult son Luke, who cannot afford a place of his own as a consequence of rising house prices(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of one of Hollywood's best-loved actresses, telling the story of her Oscar-winning career by using rarely seen archive footage of her appearances on the BBC(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profile of American actor, director, writer and producer Orson Welles, whose significant contribution to cinema included Citizen Kane and The Third Man. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Jane Eyre (1943)
programme.desc: A naive young woman becomes governess in a mysterious Yorkshire mansion and before long she is completely besotted with her charismatic but intimidating employer. But unbeknown to her, the master of the house is concealing a dark secret. Romantic period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Joan Fontaine, Orson Welles, Agnes Moorehead and Margaret O'Brien(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Croome stately home in Worcestershire, where Natasha Raskin Sharp eyes ceremonial gold epaulettes and David Fletcher comes across an unusual vase(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A land mine cuts off all power and communications services in Walmington-on-Sea, but Captain Mainwaring rises to the challenge by declaring martial law - while Pike gets stuck on some iron railings. Vintage comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe and Ian Lavender(n)

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: How the country's surrounding oceans - the Southern Sea, the Pacific and the Indian Ocean - create a unique environment for numerous creatures. In the waters off south Australia, sealions, once a rare sight, are now thriving, while in the wide shallows of Spencer Gulf, a mid-winter gathering of colourful giant cuttlefish battle for mates. Off the west coast, in the warm currents of the Indian Ocean, brown boobies build makeshift nests, while further south tiger sharks sweep in to prey on a whale carcass. Narrated by Barry Humphries(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: The final leg of Michael Portillo's journey begins in Edmonton, where he learns about the people who 300 years ago travelled huge distances by foot and canoe to trade with indigenous people. He also admires the city's early 20th-century streetcars, preserved by the Radial Railway Society, and its light rail system. Moving on, he views the Rocky Mountains, marvels at the scale of Canada's forestry industry and discovers how the beaver, once slaughtered for its fur, is now pampered(n)

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up Jamali Maddix comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee(n)

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke attempt to transform a scruffy back garden in Fife into a safe and stimulating space for an elderly man with dementia(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter C. Servings include celeriac and pomegranate salad and Raymond Blanc's cafe creme. Jamie Oliver also gives some tips(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme celebrates the humble snowdrop on a visit to one of the world's largest collections, lovingly nurtured in a suburban back garden, as Monty Don offers tips on what to do once they have finished flowering. At Longmeadow it's time to get dahlias into growth and, for inspiration, there's camera footage of a field full of dahlias in Sussex along with tips from a professional grower. Plus, Nick Bailey revels in the beauty of magnolias and Monty highlights the jobs to be getting on with this weekend(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: A cutdown edition of the show featuring tips for green-fingered beginners and seasoned enthusiasts alike(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring London and the South-east chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the one-time Blue Peter presenter Konnie Huq, whose Cookie and the Most Annoying Boy in the World adventure series is making waves with young readers(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip reaches a conclusion as he arrives in Istanbul, just in time for the bluefish season, which allows him the chance to enjoy a fishing expedition on the Bosphorus, one of the world's most strategic waterways, which divides the city into two continents. Rick also cooks two much-loved local dishes - lavish kofte kebabs with pistachios, and kapuska, a hearty traditional Turkish cuisine stew from the Black Sea region, whose name is derived from the Russian word for cabbage(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Sudan, the very last male northern white rhino on the planet. Snatched from his mother's side in Central Africa, Sudan became a prized exhibit in a zoo while the rest of his kind were poached to near-extinction. This programme focuses on the desperate battle to bring his sub-species back from the brink of extinction, told through the characters who have been involved in his life(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The first of three programmes about the wildlife, landscape and culture in different areas of the country explores the Sierra Madre mountains, and reveals how this rugged landscape has shaped the nation. This chain of peaks includes several volcanoes and is home to a wide variety of animals, including black bears, monarch butterflies, orchid bees and quetzals. The programme also visits the ruins of an ancient Aztec shrine and the blue agave plant which grows in the mountains is used in making tequila(n)

programme.title: Evil Under the Sun (1982)
programme.desc: Hercule Poirot braves the heat and sand to investigate the murder of a glamorous stage star on an idyllic Adriatic holiday island. It becomes clear many people had reason to want the temperamental actress dead and the Belgian sleuth is soon hard at work figuring out which of her enemies eventually managed to kill her. Mystery, with Peter Ustinov, Diana Rigg, James Mason, Maggie Smith and Jane Birkin(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction, and Paul Martin finds out about the history of Sledmere House in East Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: It is the day of Gemma and Kieran's wedding, but the happy couple are still reeling from Ray's heartfelt declaration the night before, and struggle to cope with their feelings. Kieran lashes out, while Ray tries to avoid drawing attention to herself - until Gemma's friends drag her into helping them get the bride ready(n)

programme.title: Jawbone (2017)
programme.desc: After hitting rock bottom, former youth boxing champion Jimmy McCabe returns to his childhood boxing club and his old team - gym owner Bill, corner-man Eddie and promoter Joe. Back in training, years after anyone thought he was a contender, he risks his life as he tries to stand tall and regain his place in the world. Drama, starring Johnny Harris, Ray Winstone, Ian McShane and Michael Smiley(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: The latest international headlines(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies host the edition first shown on June 1, featuring performances by Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry(n)

programme.title: Kings of Soul
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the male artists whose vocal stylings have played an instrumental role in the soul genre from the 60s to the 00s. Featuring footage of Brenton Wood performing Gimme Little Sign and Curtis Mayfield singing Keep On Keeping On, as well as appearances by Billy Preston, Bill Withers, Billy Ocean, Alexander O'Neal, Bobby Womack and Barry White(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on June 8, featuring performances by the Beautiful South, Guns N' Roses and Jason Donovan(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Island: Where Legends Were Born
programme.desc: The history of Eel Pie Island, a small island in the Thames in south-west London, which became the epicentre of R'n'B in the 1960s. In its heyday, the likes of the Rolling Stones, the Yardbirds, the Who, David Bowie, Elton John, Pink Floyd, Fleetwood Mac, John Mayall, Long John Baldry and many others cut their teeth at the venue before becoming legends of the music industry. Nigel Planer narrates this documentary exploring the unique experiences of the people who either played at the Island Hotel or went there to listen to music and dance on its famous bouncing dance floor. Contributors include Rod Stewart, Steve Hackett and Dave Brock(n)

programme.title: Sgt Pepper's Musical Revolution - with Howard Goodall
programme.desc: The composer explores why the Beatles' 50-year-old album is still revered as an innovative, revolutionary and influential release. With the help of out-takes, studio conversations between the band and never-heard-before outside of Abbey Road, Howard gets 'under the bonnet' of the album, taking the music apart and reassembling it to reveal how it works. Producer George Martin and his team constructed the album sound by sound, layer by layer - a formula that became the norm for just about every rock act who followed(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Examination examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words in archived interviews, along with commentary from those people closest to him. This episode focuses on Sinatra's birth to his early years as a roadhouse performer, revealing the influences behind his meteoric rise. Introduced by Matt Goss(n)

programme.title: Acoustic at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of acoustic guitar performances from the BBC archives, featuring David Bowie's Starman, Bert Jansch, Johnny Marr and Bernard Butler with The River Bank, Chelsea Morning by Joni Mitchell and Wonderwall by Oasis. Also included is Jimmy Page's TV debut from 1958, as well as a rendition of Mountains by Biffy Clyro(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music public relations expert Alan Edwards tells the story of why so many bands are getting back together, what happens when they do - and how such reunions have changed the music business. He starts by recalling the UK's first revival concert, which took place over 40 years ago at Wembley Stadium. Featuring Bill Hailey and the Comets, Little Richard and Jerry Lee Lewis - the event opened the eyes of promoters to the power of yesterday's hit-makers to reach an audience and make money. From there, Alan takes on a musical journey through some of the biggest reunions of the last 30 years. Contributors include Glen Matlock, Debbie Harry, Stewart Copeland and Melanie C(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Island: Where Legends Were Born
programme.desc: The history of Eel Pie Island, a small island in the Thames in south-west London, which became the epicentre of R'n'B in the 1960s. In its heyday, the likes of the Rolling Stones, the Yardbirds, the Who, David Bowie, Elton John, Pink Floyd, Fleetwood Mac, John Mayall, Long John Baldry and many others cut their teeth at the venue before becoming legends of the music industry. Nigel Planer narrates this documentary exploring the unique experiences of the people who either played at the Island Hotel or went there to listen to music and dance on its famous bouncing dance floor. Contributors include Rod Stewart, Steve Hackett and Dave Brock(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to the north-eastern Greek city of Thessaloniki, which nestles on the northern shores of the Aegean Sea. The city is revered as the gourmet capital of Greece and birthplace to possibly the greatest warrior general that ever lived - Alexander. Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. Back home, he prepares veal and aubergine stew after meeting the doyenne of Greek cooking, Vafa Alexiadou(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop present an exhaustive guide to East Anglia, from the Wash to Canvey Island. Neil takes to the seas to record new stories, and revisits some of the best Coast features from the past 10 years as he visits England's biggest grey seal colony, and witnesses extreme erosion at Happisburgh. He also takes a trip on board a floating relic of the herring-fishing industry, and finishes his exploration at Orford Ness, where one of the region's most distinctive landmarks is at risk of becoming a victim of the constant battle between land and sea(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: The detective's quiet retreat with Livia comes to an unexpected end when he is called back to Vigata to investigate the murder of a 70-year-old prostitute, who has been strangled. Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Shanghai
programme.desc: In a programme filmed in May 1989, Clive set out to find out about a city that was once known as the Paris of the East and discovered China on the brink of revolution. As demonstrating students joined three million cyclists to clog the streets, he encounters a once-wealthy socialite who survived the Cultural Revolution, a baby-washing entrepreneur, a local eel-gutter and a jazz band playing Jingle Bells on an empty ferry cruising up the Huangpu River(n)

programme.title: Lost Sitcoms
programme.desc: A remake of an episode from 1970. Harold is planning on taking a skiing holiday - but he does not want dad Albert there under any circumstances. Jeff Rawle and Ed Coleman star(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies host the edition first shown on June 1, featuring performances by Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on June 8, featuring performances by the Beautiful South, Guns N' Roses and Jason Donovan(n)

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Stephen and Gillian have been married for 24 years and been in New Order together for even longer, but they still manage to surprise one another with their musical tastes. While Stephen declares Captain Beefheart an early influence, Gillian confesses her teenage love for a disco classic. Amidst an hour of top tunes, Stephen also reveals the moment he was mistaken for Stevie Wonder and Gillian recalls how her father was a fan of punk. From Kraftwerk to Can, David Bowie to Kate Bush, Magazine to Grace Jones and many more, their stellar playlist is brimming with iconic performances(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to the north-eastern Greek city of Thessaloniki, which nestles on the northern shores of the Aegean Sea. The city is revered as the gourmet capital of Greece and birthplace to possibly the greatest warrior general that ever lived - Alexander. Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. Back home, he prepares veal and aubergine stew after meeting the doyenne of Greek cooking, Vafa Alexiadou(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Waterlands: Escape to Swallows and Amazons Country
programme.desc: Presenters Dick Strawbridge and Professor Alice Roberts explore the British landscapes that inspired children's author Arthur Ransome to write his famous series Swallows and Amazons. Strawbridge uses vintage boats to investigate three iconic waterlands - the Lake District, the Norfolk broads, and the Suffolk coastline - while Roberts takes a look at the shore lines(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong build a wooden boat, following original designs for the vessels used during the Gold Rush. This vessel must carry them across hundreds of miles of treacherous lakes and rivers, including the infamous One Mile Rapids, a narrow canyon of white water and jagged rocks(n)

programme.title: The Odyssey (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic covering 30 years in the life of acclaimed oceanographer and filmmaker Jacques-Yves Cousteau. Starring Lambert Wilson, Pierre Niney and Audrey Tautou. In French(n)

programme.title: Francis Bacon: A Brush with Violence
programme.desc: Documentary exploring just how much of Francis Bacon's notoriety as a rude, drunken loudmouth was a carefully curated front he maintained to protect the complex and haunted man behind his own myth. Making use of interviews with celebrity commentators, fellow artists, and some of the people who knew him best, this programme sets out to deconstruct the perceived and hidden identities of the man known as one of the most sought-after British artists of the 20th century(n)
